mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0% signals labor market normalization, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in >50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low federal funds rates and stable consumer price inflation compress real discount rates while preserving purchasing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative Rank IC when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY change < 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and aggregate supply, which supports corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during supply-driven recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns decline when INDPRO rises but CPIAUCSL rises concurrently by >0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 500,000 units annually signal sustained consumer confidence and construction activity, boosting related sector earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where HOUST exceeds 500.0.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening aggregate demand and wage growth, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window despite consistent payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accommodative federal funds rates indicate robust consumer spending supported by cheap credit, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails when RSAFS declines but FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, producing negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4% triggers inflation fears, prompting rate hike expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where CPIAUCSL monthly change exceeds 0.4%.
